column1: Copied from the latest tailored AI Readiness content pack.

column3: Whether the use case moves a meaningful commercial or operational KPI rather than saving only trivial effort.
column4: Solves a minor convenience issue with no KPI or customer impact.
column5: Creates a local team benefit only; impact is limited or hard to notice in reporting.
column6: Improves a relevant workflow metric but the commercial upside is still moderate or indirect.
column7: Materially improves a departmental KPI such as conversion, SLA, turnaround time, or reporting cycle time.
column8: Directly affects a top business KPI such as revenue conversion, retention, margin protection, cash collection, or service reliability.
column9: KPI link, affected team size, customer or cash impact, cost of delay, and volume of affected work

column3: Whether the workflow is stable enough and the operating model clear enough to implement without redesigning the business first.
column4: Workflow is unstable, undocumented, or ownership is unclear.
column5: Use case is understandable but process variation, exceptions, or ownership gaps are still major blockers.
column6: Workflow is partly defined and could support a narrow pilot with active manual oversight.
column7: Workflow is mostly stable with known inputs, outputs, and review points.
column8: Workflow is already disciplined, repeatable, and implementation can happen without major redesign.
column9: Workflow documentation, owner clarity, exception rate, SOP maturity, and pilot control design

column3: Whether the inputs needed for the use case actually exist, are accessible, and are good enough to trust outputs.
column4: Required data is missing, inaccessible, or too poor to support the use case.
column5: Some inputs exist but there are major gaps, manual gathering, or poor quality problems.
column6: Enough data exists for a scoped pilot, but cleanup or supplementation is still needed.
column7: Most required data is available, accessible, and reasonably reliable.
column8: Required data is already captured cleanly, consistently, and at enough history depth for the use case.
column9: Input fields, document access, transcript quality, source-of-truth confidence, and data ownership

column3: How easily the output can be embedded into the workflow or system where teams already work.
column4: No practical insertion point exists; output would stay outside the workflow.
column5: Embedding is possible only through heavy manual copy-paste or workaround steps.
column6: A pilot can run with some manual handling, but workflow embedding is still partial.
column7: There is a workable integration or handoff path into the user's existing system.
column8: Output can be embedded directly into the operational workflow with low technical friction.
column9: API/connectors, handoff points, system of record, queue insertion, and technical owner availability

column3: How safely the use case can be piloted given data sensitivity, customer exposure, and error consequences. Higher score = lower risk.
column4: High-risk use case touching sensitive data or critical external decisions with weak controls.
column5: Material risk remains and would require strong manual review on every output.
column6: Moderate risk; usable in pilot with defined controls and human review.
column7: Low-to-moderate risk with manageable review requirements and clear rollback options.
column8: Low-risk use case with contained consequences, strong guardrails, and easy human override.
column9: Data sensitivity, customer-facing exposure, compliance impact, reversibility, and review requirement

column3: How quickly the business could see a measurable benefit once work starts.
column4: Would take long preparation with little near-term return.
column5: Meaningful setup is required before any benefit is visible.
column6: Pilot benefit could appear in a medium-term window after some foundation work.
column7: Value should become visible relatively quickly after a controlled pilot starts.
column8: Fast path to observable benefit because workflow, data, and deployment route are already close to ready.
column9: Implementation lead time, dependencies, stakeholder load, and speed of KPI movement
